history.qwen_observation: UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 partially migrated Spring Security configuration, renaming antMatchers to requestMatchers but leaving authorizeRequests unchanged, which causes a compilation error since authorizeRequests() no longer returns a type with requestMatchers() in Spring Security 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding SpringBoot3BestPractices to the Spring Boot 3 upgrade step helps catch remaining migration gaps and enforces Spring Security 6 API compliance, including the required authorizeRequests to authorizeHttpRequests rename that the base meta-recipe sometimes misses in non-standard configurations.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the git-commit-id-plugin threw a TransportException (Missing delta base) while attempting to fetch git data in a shallow or corrupted CI clone.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding a Maven property to skip the git-commit-id-plugin will bypass the network/git fetch failure, allowing the Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe to apply and compile successfully. The chain then proceeds with standard Java 21 plugin and build upgrades, relying on the meta-recipe to handle Spring Security 6 API migrations automatically.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed because Spring Security 6 API migration (authorizeRequests -> authorizeHttpRequests) was not applied by UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3, leaving deprecated method calls that no longer exist in the upgraded dependencies.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding SpringBoot3BestPractices alongside UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 ensures that Spring Security 6 code migrations are explicitly applied during the same step, fixing the authorizeRequests/requestMatchers compilation errors before the build validates the code.

history.qwen_observation: Git fetch failure in git-commit-id-plugin blocks recipe application, and Spring Security 6 API migration may be incomplete.
history.qwen_rationale: Setting multiple git skip properties ensures the plugin is disabled during the build phase triggered by OpenRewrite, preventing the TransportException from aborting the migration. The Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe will then proceed to handle the javax->jakarta and Spring Security 6 migrations.

history.qwen_observation: The git-commit-id-plugin fails during recipe application due to a shallow clone delta error, and Spring Security 6 API migration is incomplete.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding robust git skip properties (git.skip, git.failOnUnableToExtractRepoInfo) in the first step prevents the plugin from running during the build phase. Keeping UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 and SpringBoot3BestPractices ensures the Spring Security 6 migration (authorizeHttpRequests/requestMatchers) is applied correctly before bumping to Java 21.
